digest.component_or_area: MCP trusted-proxy auth verifier + header-guard middleware
propositions: Add a trusted-proxy auth mode activated via the 'proxy' entry in mcp.auth_strategies that delegates authentication to a fronting edge proxy.
An ASGI middleware validates a shared-secret header with constant-time compare and only then extracts the identity header into a request-scoped principal, otherwise stripping it.

The middleware strips the entire X-Forwarded-* family plus configured identity/secret headers on requests lacking a valid secret, normalizing case and underscore/dash variants to defeat header smuggling.

A TokenVerifier-shaped component mints an AccessToken with client_id=identity, scopes=auth_proxy_scopes, and resource=auth_resource_server_url so it passes the same composite audience check, returning None when no verified identity is present.
The proxy verifier fails closed: it refuses to start if the env var named by auth_proxy_secret_env is absent or empty, and never accepts identity on a missing/invalid secret.
auth_proxy_scopes defaults to an empty set so a proxy principal holds no scopes and is denied 403 by any non-empty auth_required_scopes until scopes are configured.
